caption: The video appears to be a screen recording of an IRS webpage discussing required minimum distributions (RMDs) for individuals over the age of 72. The page highlights that those born in 1950 or earlier must start withdrawing funds from their retirement plans and individual retirement arrangements by certain deadlines to avoid penalties. It explains that RMDs are the minimum amounts that many retirement plan and IRA account owners must withdraw annually after reaching age 72. Account holders can delay their first RMD until April 1 following the year they turn 72, but subsequent distributions must be taken by December 31 each year. The text also mentions specific types of IRAs, such as traditional, SEP, SARSEP, and SIMPLE, which are subject to these rules. The cursor is visible on the screen, indicating that someone is navigating through the information. The video likely serves as an informational guide for viewers to understand their financial obligations regarding RMDs.
